code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test06-SEP-2020Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with abnormal urine appearance and low specific gravity, indicating possible renal tubular dysfunction and/or acute metabolic disturbance. HbA1c is low, arguing against chronic hyperglycemia. Findings warrant further evaluation for acute kidney injury, possible hepatic involvement, and metabolic derangements. Clinical correlation is recommended.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
